selected_important_turn.text: Oh I wouldn't want for you to freeze☹️ It also gets cold where I am, so I need 2 firewood as well.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the clear escalation point. The opponent had just explained why they need 2 firewood; by immediately claiming the same scarce resource the agent turned an informational, integrative exchange into direct competition. That move precipitated the opponent's later ultimatum and withdrawal, so it strongly shifted the negotiation trajectory toward breakdown.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although expressing empathy is good, coupling it with an identical hard claim created zero-sum tension. The turn is improvable because it missed an opportunity to preserve cooperation by proposing a concrete compromise or trade rather than matching the demand. It increased scarcity framing and closed off integrative options.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Maintain the empathetic tone but follow with a concrete, generous-seeming compromise or trade proposal (e.g., propose splitting the two firewood 1/1, offer 1 firewood in exchange for extra water or another concession, or ask if one firewood would suffice). Avoid immediately claiming the full same entitlement; instead frame a mutual solution and offer alternatives.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — a concrete compromise would keep options open and preserve value by avoiding walk-away.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — proposing a split or trade is more equitable than both demanding the full amount.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a specific compromise lowers resistance and reduces the chance of ultimatums.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — reduces perceived competition and builds trust by showing willingness to trade/share.

value2reason.Low: We do a lot of hiking while camping
value2reason.Medium: It gets very cold at night
value2reason.High: I am bringing a big group of people camping

value2reason.Low: I would like more food to feed the hungry people I came across while camping
value2reason.Medium: It is unusually cold for this time of year, and I need extra firewood to keep the fire going
value2reason.High: I need more water to give to the stranded people I found while hiking by the camp

dialogue.text: Hi. Water is very important to me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hello, I don't really need water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: What is it that you need?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need water and then firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: You can have all the water if I can have all the extra food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: OK, but first tell me why you need extra food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I have 10 people in my group
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see. I need more water because I came across some people who were lost and they are very dehydrated.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need 2 firewood because it gets below freezing at night
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Oh I wouldn't want for you to freeze☹️ It also gets cold where I am, so I need 2 firewood as well.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for firewood. Let's see—maybe I can offer you 1 firewood package in exchange for 1 water package. That way, we both get what we need without competing over the same resource. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with that trade. Firewood is important for the group, and I need water for hiking. Let’s agree on 1 firewood for 1 water. What else do you need to finalize the split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That sounds fair. Let me propose a full split: I’ll take 2 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood, while you get 1 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. This way, we both meet our needs and share the resources. Does that work for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that split works for me. I’ll take 1 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. Let’s confirm and then wrap this up.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Great! Let’s confirm the final split: I’ll take 2 water, 1 food, and 2 firewood, while you get 1 water, 2 food, and 1 firewood. All packages are accounted for, and we’re good to go.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
